mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL above its trailing 3-month average reflects accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL is above its 3-month average.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 4.5% from below, labor market deterioration produces recession risk repricing and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in RSAFS and month-over-month rise in FEDFUNDS reflect demand destruction amid tightening liquidity and produce strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term does not improve predictive R-squared over additive single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO during periods when UNRATE is below 5.0% reflects supply-constrained capacity rather than weak demand, prompting firms to raise prices and expand margins, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than half of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in HOUST reflects accelerating residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases over 3 months coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, further increases in the federal funds rate directly raise the risk-free discount rate and reduce the present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS above 3.0% and rising are associated with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS and deceleration in UNRATE reflect a labor market absorption rate that outpaces labor force growth, creating wage-price spiral fears that compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The simultaneous signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power in a regression.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDPC1 below its trailing 4-quarter average reflects negative output gap emergence and earnings downgrade momentum, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 below its trailing 4-quarter average coincides with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of subsequent quarters.
